citation.description: AGENTS.md instructs contributors to use their own real, configured git identity; bot identity scoped to machine-local config only. CONTRIBUTING.md §DCO (lines 198-218) is identity-neutral. scripts/check_dco_identity.py enforces no hardcoded 'Joe Oakhart' / 'joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com' in contributor-facing guidance via make lint.

criteria.criterion: Contributor and agent guidance uses each contributor's configured real DCO identity; Joe Oakhart's bot identity appears only where an explicitly automated workflow requires it.

citation.description: Library docstring: 'force bypasses GATES generally — including any gate added in the future — but it does not waive the unresolved-children close prohibition: that is a ticket-system invariant, not a gate'. CLI help (transition.py:43-46) and claim.py:30 use identical future-gate language. AGENTS.md:63-66 also states force bypasses 'plan-review or whatever gate is configured, present or future'.

criteria.criterion: Public transition guidance states that force bypasses all gates, including future gates, while ticket-system invariants such as the open-children close prohibition remain non-bypassable.

citation.description: An empty raw_output from a chunk with non-empty input extends empty_outcome_indices and increments outcome_counts['empty_outcomes']; those findings degrade to INDETERMINATE (no verifications returned), never reaching a clean PASS. structured.py lines 140-167 provides fence-extraction + direct parse + first-object + json_repair chain for provider-independent self-healing parsing.

criteria.criterion: Verification preserves flexible provider-independent parsing and self-healing, but no semantically empty result can be accepted as a valid review outcome.

citation.description: finalize_verdict checks 'elif coverage.get("hierarchy_incomplete"): verdict = "INDETERMINATE"' before the PASS fallthrough, with comment 'No relevance-exception carve-out'. Hierarchy-incomplete detection with bounded retry (2 attempts, 50ms) at lines 184-219 sets hierarchy_incomplete=True on enumeration or per-child fetch exhaustion.

criteria.criterion: Plan review cannot produce an ordinary clean pass when the ticket hierarchy was loaded incompletely, unless the omitted context is demonstrably irrelevant.

citation.description: docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md covers: 6-dimension capability check (local checkout, rebar binary, tracker worktree, network, GitHub connector, Gerrit); Section 2 (GitHub is read-only mirror, Gerrit is where code lands); Section 4 (fallback when native ticket creation impossible — which fires when tracker + network/binary unavailable); Section 5 (exceptional import via rebar import only). AGENTS.md §120-122 points to this doc.

criteria.criterion: ChatGPT-specific guidance explains how to use Rebar safely when the GitHub repository is a read-only mirror and native clone, Gerrit, or tracker access is unavailable.
